rag_rules: When repairing AGREE contracts, ensure that all identifiers used in assumptions and guarantees are visible in the current scope and properly declared.
Place component type contracts in valid component type scopes, avoiding implementation-level bindings in type contracts.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex when defining AGREE assumptions and guarantees.
Validate that all referenced identifiers resolve to valid expressions with known types before adding clauses to contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE guarantees must reference valid expressions; avoid clauses with unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations.
Component type contracts should define input assumptions and output guarantees using concrete bounds or named constants.
anti_patterns: Do not add assumptions or guarantees that reference identifiers not visible in the current scope or that are not declared in the same annex.
Avoid inserting implementation-level bindings into component type contracts; these belong in implementation scopes.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package or property set visibility
